factorization_attempts.interpretation: T23 quantum redundancy reduction factors through a one-site local D1 structure, but this is not a full field representation theorem.
factorization_attempts.name: local_quantum_redundancy_reduction

factorization_attempts.interpretation: The weak-quorum case is an informative failed morphism: the obstruction must be reported rather than represented as preserved transport.
factorization_attempts.name: weak_quorum_boundary

factorization_attempts.interpretation: Full representation is not attempted as a success claim because T23 IPTs do not yet carry site maps or restriction-map commutation data.
factorization_attempts.name: full_ipt_representation

hypotheses: H3: another finite local-to-global structure is required
H4: no canonical generalization is currently justified

evidence_for: scalar recovery theorem succeeds in uniform/fixed-observer cases
hypothesis_evaluations.verdict: retain scalar D1 only as local or explicitly aggregated special case

evidence_against: same vector can have different transport reachability
evidence_for: vector sufficiency theorem succeeds in trusted-chain divergent-profile case
hypothesis_evaluations.verdict: use vector D1 only for observer-distribution snapshots

evidence_against: full sheaf language not required by finite evidence
hypothesis_evaluations.verdict: field-like data is required when transport or gluing is in scope

hypothesis_evaluations.claim: Another finite local-to-global structure is required.
evidence_against: not yet tested on continuous/covariant domains
evidence_for: finite patch constraints handle gluing obstruction
hypothesis_evaluations.verdict: best current object is a finite graph-indexed D1 restriction system

hypothesis_evaluations.claim: No canonical generalization is currently justified.
hypothesis_evaluations.evidence_against: finite graph-restriction structure explains all T25 cases with less loss than scalar/vector
evidence_for: full sheaf and full IPT representation are deferred
hypothesis_evaluations.verdict: too pessimistic for current finite evidence, but remains a boundary for broader physics claims

negative_results.failed_claim: one selected or aggregate profile explains multiscale finality
negative_results.framework_boundary: scalar D1 remains valid only for fixed observer or uniform-field assumptions
negative_results.reflects: mathematical limit of scalar aggregation for cross-observer claims
negative_results.what_failed: componentwise scalar reductions erase observer distribution
negative_results.why_it_failed: stratified_access_delay has live local/lab finality and institutional non-finality

negative_results.framework_boundary: vector D1 remains valid for snapshots where graph transport is out of scope
negative_results.what_failed: same vector has different trust-path reachability
negative_results.why_it_failed: connected_same_vector and partitioned_same_vector have identical vectors but different graphs

negative_results.failed_claim: locally satisfiable finality patches always globalize
negative_results.framework_boundary: local finality does not imply global finality
negative_results.why_it_failed: contextual_gluing_obstruction encodes three same constraints and one different constraint

negative_results.failed_claim: all current IPTs factor through D1 local-to-global structure
negative_results.framework_boundary: boundary: local factorization is supported; full representation remains unearned
negative_results.reflects: implementation boundary and mathematical open problem
negative_results.what_failed: current IPT type lacks restriction-map commutation data
negative_results.why_it_failed: T23 was built as typed invariant transport, not as a field morphism system

question_zero_answer: The smallest earned abstraction is a finite graph-indexed local-to-global D1 restriction system: local D1 profiles, observer sites, trusted transport edges, and optional patch constraints. Scalar and vector views are recoverable fragments; full sheaf language and full IPT representation are not yet earned.
recommendation: Adopt a finite graph-indexed local-to-global D1 restriction system as the minimal next formal object. Retain scalar D1 for fixed-observer or uniform cases, use vector D1 when only observer distribution matters, use graph restriction data when transport or gluing matters, and defer full sheaf and full IPT representation claims pending stronger evidence.

structure_audits.verdict: too weak for existing multiscale evidence
sufficient only when observer distribution matters but transport/gluing do not
smallest sufficient finite abstraction in this audit
promising but not required by the finite evidence yet

information_loss: restriction-map semantics absent from current IPT type
structure_audits.verdict: not yet earned; local factorization is positive but full theorem is deferred

theorem_ladder.failure_or_boundary: does not apply to divergent observer profiles
theorem_ladder.name: Scalar Recovery Theorem
theorem_ladder.statement: A scalar D1 profile is recovered without material loss when local profiles are uniform, trusted transport is connected, and there are no gluing constraints.

evidence: scalar loses observer distribution but graph adds no new distinction
theorem_ladder.failure_or_boundary: does not apply when graph connectivity, trust, or gluing changes the result
theorem_ladder.name: Vector Sufficiency Theorem
theorem_ladder.statement: Vector D1 is sufficient when observer profiles diverge but transport is trusted/connected and no gluing constraints are present.

theorem_ladder.failure_or_boundary: does not require sheaf language; graph reachability is enough here
theorem_ladder.name: Transport Necessity Theorem
theorem_ladder.statement: Graph transport data is required when two cases have the same observer-profile vector but different trust-path reachability.

evidence: local witnesses exist; global witness count is zero
theorem_ladder.failure_or_boundary: finite constraint obstruction only; not a full sheaf theorem
theorem_ladder.name: Gluing Obstruction Theorem
theorem_ladder.statement: Local D1-compatible patches can be individually satisfiable while no global assignment exists.

theorem_ladder.failure_or_boundary: only local one-site factorization was earned
theorem_ladder.name: Morphism Theorem
theorem_ladder.statement: A local IPT can factor through a one-site D1 restriction system when it preserves declared local D1 invariants.

evidence: not enough structure in current IPT object
theorem_ladder.name: IPT Representation Theorem
theorem_ladder.statement: A full IPT representation theorem would require site maps, restriction-map commutation, and obstruction preservation for all relevant IPTs.
